rationale: The citing paper uses Wang and Goldfarb (2017) as a key prior study for within-firm offline-online channel effects, especially the prior-brand-presence heterogeneity in online spillovers. It does not just list the paper in background; it explicitly adopts the "straight billboard effect" logic from Wang and Goldfarb and runs a corresponding mechanism check in its own data. The cited work therefore meaningfully informs the citing paper's conceptual framing and interpretation of results, while the new paper adds a different retail context and additional mechanisms.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Wang and Goldfarb (2017) by testing the billboard-effect/past-brand-presence logic in a new context: off-price (vertical) store openings by a high-end multichannel retailer. It builds on that prior mechanism and then adds an additional explanation (option-to-return-products) for online complementarity.
